description:
You are a cloud-architecture consultant who has been asked to reorganise a legacy configuration layout so that it is ready for an upcoming multi-region migration.  
A set of legacy “*.conf” files currently lives in “/home/user/old_configs”.  The target design keeps the real files under “/home/user/cloud_migration/configs” while leaving backward-compatible symbolic links in their original place.

Your goal:
1. Physically move every “*.conf” file that is directly inside “/home/user/old_configs” to “/home/user/cloud_migration/configs”.  
2. Replace each moved file in “/home/user/old_configs” with a **symbolic link** of the same name that points to its new absolute path.  
3. Create the directory “/home/user/migration_logs” (if it does not yet exist).  
4. Generate a one-line-per-file audit report named “/home/user/migration_logs/symlink_audit.log”.

Exact audit-log format (must match **character-for-character**, including spaces and punctuation, one entry per line, terminated with a “\n” newline):

<original-filename> -> <absolute-target-path> : OK

Requirements & checks that the automated grader will perform:

• Only symbolic links should remain under “/home/user/old_configs”; no regular copies of the “*.conf” files should still be there.  
• Every link’s target **must** be “/home/user/cloud_migration/configs/<same-file>” and must exist.  
• The audit file must list a line for each migrated file, sorted lexicographically by file name (e.g. “app.conf” appears before “auth.conf”, etc.).  
• There must be exactly one space before and after “->”, exactly one space before and after “:”, and the word “OK” must be in uppercase.  
• Example of a correctly-formatted line (do NOT wrap):  
  app.conf -> /home/user/cloud_migration/configs/app.conf : OK

If everything is done properly you will have:
• A new directory tree at “/home/user/cloud_migration/configs” holding the real “*.conf” files.  
• Matching symbolic links inside “/home/user/old_configs”.  
• A verification file at “/home/user/migration_logs/symlink_audit.log” whose contents strictly follow the specified format.

The entire task can be completed with roughly 5–10 standard shell commands.  Work entirely within the unprivileged user environment; no sudo is required.
truth:
Initial state provided to the agent:

Directory /home/user/old_configs exists and contains four regular files:

1. /home/user/old_configs/app.conf
   Content (including trailing newline):
   APP_ENV=production\n

2. /home/user/old_configs/auth.conf
   Content:
   AUTH_METHOD=oauth2\n

3. /home/user/old_configs/cache.conf
   Content:
   CACHE_SIZE=256\n

4. /home/user/old_configs/db.conf
   Content:
   DB_HOST=localhost\n

No other “*.conf” files exist anywhere else.

Expected final state for verification:

A. Directory tree:
/home/user/cloud_migration/configs/            (directory)
/home/user/migration_logs/                     (directory)

B. Real configuration files (regular files, contents unchanged) now located at:
/home/user/cloud_migration/configs/app.conf
/home/user/cloud_migration/configs/auth.conf
/home/user/cloud_migration/configs/cache.conf
/home/user/cloud_migration/configs/db.conf

C. Symbolic links (type “l”) inside /home/user/old_configs:
/home/user/old_configs/app.conf    -> /home/user/cloud_migration/configs/app.conf
/home/user/old_configs/auth.conf   -> /home/user/cloud_migration/configs/auth.conf
/home/user/old_configs/cache.conf  -> /home/user/cloud_migration/configs/cache.conf
/home/user/old_configs/db.conf     -> /home/user/cloud_migration/configs/db.conf

All links must use absolute paths exactly as shown (no “../” or “~/” shortcuts).

D. Audit log:
/home/user/migration_logs/symlink_audit.log   (regular file, mode 0644)

Exact file content (with final newline and no extra blank lines):

app.conf -> /home/user/cloud_migration/configs/app.conf : OK
auth.conf -> /home/user/cloud_migration/configs/auth.conf : OK
cache.conf -> /home/user/cloud_migration/configs/cache.conf : OK
db.conf -> /home/user/cloud_migration/configs/db.conf : OK\n

(Notice lexicographic order: app.conf, auth.conf, cache.conf, db.conf)

E. After completion, there must be:
• 4 symbolic links (not regular files) in /home/user/old_configs  
• 0 regular “*.conf” files left in /home/user/old_configs  

The automated test will verify:  
1. Type of each entry (symlink vs file).  
2. Exact link targets.  
3. Exact content of the audit log, including ordering, spacing, punctuation and trailing newline.  
4. Integrity of the moved configuration files (byte-for-byte identical to originals).
